baseline.commit_subject: Add Mira Core Phase 18 persistence audit validation
baseline.baseline_boundary: Phase 18 defines persistence-schema and audit-log validation only. No real Mira Core server database, KMS integration, envelope encryption runtime, key custody, network path, queue, local execution, send, deploy, trade, memory/profile commit, export, deletion, restore, encryption, decryption, or raw-content storage exists at this baseline.

purpose: Convert the next Phase 18 migration gate, encryption/key-reference/secret handling, into an executable validation contract before any real KMS, envelope encryption, key custody, encryption, decryption, secret read, or server storage runtime exists. Phase 19 defines key references, envelope metadata, secret-handling boundaries, rotation/revocation/rewrap rules, delete/export/backup/restore constraints, capability boundaries, migration gates, and side-effect truth.
nonGoals: No network listener, network request, or KMS call.
No database, source-store, or file write.
No auth, token, credential, secret, or session change.
No token, cookie, or session secret material.
No environment secret read or export.
No browser, window, screenshot, or OCR access.
No external sends or customer sends.

scope: Build validation-only encryption/key-reference assessment records against this fixture, if Builder is later delegated.

Do not start a server, perform network or KMS work, read env secrets, generate keys, encrypt, decrypt, write databases/stores/files, run migrations, create queues, mutate auth/session/token state, execute locally, send, deploy, trade, commit memory/profile state, export a file, delete records, restore records, or store raw content.
Represent commit 3742ab7 as the Phase 18 baseline and require the persistence-audit fixture as a dependency.

Emit baseline/dependency refs, key_reference_policy, encryption_plan, secret_handling_boundary, rotation_revocation_rewrap_rules, delete_export_backup_restore_rules, capability_boundary, migration_gates, gate summary, blocker summary, evidence refs, and side-effect truth sections.
Use key references and envelope metadata only; key_id, key_version, provider_ref, payload_hash, AAD fields, and ciphertext/test-vector placeholders must never include secret material.

Allow only reference-only algorithms and placeholder ciphertext/test vectors; plaintext keys, data keys, raw payloads, real ciphertext, env secrets, and decrypted content must remain unavailable to the model.

Treat encryption/key validation as planning validation only, never as authorization for storage writes, execution, raw restore, external side effects, direct Builder/Oracle targeting, model-processing proof, or bridge green.
hardRejects: Any server process, network request/listener, KMS call, database/store/file write, migration execution, queue write, auth/secret/token/session change, env secret read, key generation, encryption, decryption, local execution, shell/PTY, browser/window access, send, customer send, deploy, trade, financial action, durable migration, memory/profile commit, export-file write, deletion, restore, or raw-content storage occurs.

Any fixture or validation output contains private key material, data key material, plaintext key material, raw tokens, cookies, session secrets, env secrets, decrypted raw content, raw comms, raw terminal, screenshot/OCR, browser state, customer private data, side-profile content, raw database payload, raw plaintext payload, or raw ciphertext payload.
A revoked, expired, stale, mismatched-scope, mismatched-profile, mismatched-device, mismatched-session, unknown-provider, unsupported-algorithm, or missing-audit key reference is accepted as usable.

A backup or export manifest exposes raw content, secret material, plaintext, data keys, raw ciphertext, or unredacted private payloads instead of redacted summaries and encrypted-reference-only metadata.
Tombstones, deletion policy, replay/watermark rules, or profile isolation can be bypassed by rotation, rewrap, restore, backup, or export planning.

Encryption validity is treated as authorization for local execution, database writes, storage, Builder/Oracle direct server targeting, Tier 2+ work, customer sends, deploys, trades, memory/profile commits, bridge green, model-processing proof, raw restore, or resurrection of deleted local state.

Phase 19 migration language implies real KMS or envelope encryption can exist before KMS choice, key custody, rotation runbook, audit logging, disaster recovery, tenant isolation, red-team leakage tests, replay/watermark compatibility, and operator controls are reviewed.

staticValidationRules.must: encryption_key_assessment.baseline_commit and validation_report.baseline_commit are exactly 3742ab7, the Phase 18 persistence-audit baseline.

Phase 19 emits validation records only; it does not start a server, perform network or KMS work, write stores/files, run migrations, create queues, mutate auth/session/token state, read env secrets, generate keys, encrypt, decrypt, execute locally, send, deploy, trade, commit memory/profile data, export, delete, restore, or store raw content.
The assessment pins commit 3742ab7 and records the Phase 18 persistence-audit contract as a validation-only dependency, not a real DB or audit runtime.

key_reference_policy includes tenant/profile/device/session scope, key_id, key_version, provider_ref, algorithm allowlist, status, rotation/revocation metadata, provenance refs, audit refs, and no secret material.
Only reference-only envelope algorithms from the fixture allowlist are accepted; unsupported, production-looking, missing, or unknown-provider algorithms fail closed.

encryption_plan contains envelope metadata only: payload hash, canonicalization version, AAD fields, schema/table/item scope, key refs, and ciphertext/test-vector placeholders with no plaintext, data key, raw payload, real ciphertext, encryption, or decryption.
Private keys, data keys, tokens, cookies, session secrets, env secrets, plaintext payloads, decrypted raw content, and model-visible decrypted content are unavailable.
Rotation, revocation, and rewrap are dry-run/planned only; revoked, expired, mismatched, stale, unknown-provider, or missing-audit key refs fail closed.
Deletion policy and tombstones take precedence; exports and backups are redacted encrypted-reference-only manifests, and restore does not decrypt, resurrect, or expose raw content.

Encryption validity does not authorize local execution, DB writes, storage writes, Builder/Oracle direct server targeting, Tier 2+ action, customer-send, deploy, trade, memory/profile commit, model-processing proof, bridge green, raw restore, or resurrection of deleted state.

Real KMS/envelope encryption remains blocked until KMS choice, key custody, rotation runbook, audit logging, disaster recovery, tenant isolation, red-team leakage tests, replay/watermark compatibility, operator controls, and secret storage policy exist.
All Phase 19 side-effect truth fields are true/zero-equivalent in assessment and validation report.
Tombstones and deletion policy win over key rotation, rewrap, backup, export, restore, stale upload, and replay planning.
All required Phase 19 acceptance gate IDs and blocker IDs are present and unique.

Assessment and validation output contain no secret-like strings, key material, plaintext, raw ciphertext, raw private content, KMS/network/write/encrypt/decrypt claims, bridge/proof overclaims, direct Builder/Oracle target authorization, Tier 2+ authorization claims, or live side-effect claims.

summaryAcceptanceCriteria: Phase 19 depends on Phase 18 persistence-audit validation and does not claim a real DB, audit runtime, KMS, encryption, or decryption path exists.

Phase 19 is validation/spec-only and performs no runtime, server, network, KMS, database/store/file write, migration, queue, auth/session/token mutation, env secret read, key generation, encryption, decryption, local execution, send, deploy, trade, memory/profile commit, export, deletion, restore, raw-content storage, or output-file side effect.
Encryption-key output shape includes encryption_key_assessment and validation_report.

key_reference_policy includes tenant/profile/device/session scope, key_id, key_version, provider_ref, algorithm allowlist, status, rotation/revocation metadata, provenance refs, audit refs, and no secret material.
Algorithm allowlist is reference-only and unsupported, production-looking, missing, or unknown-provider algorithms fail closed.

encryption_plan is envelope metadata only with payload hash, canonicalization version, AAD fields, schema/table/item scope, key refs, ciphertext/test-vector placeholders, and no plaintext, data key, raw payload, real ciphertext, encryption, or decryption.
secret_handling_boundary blocks private keys, data keys, tokens, cookies, session secrets, env secrets, plaintext payloads, decrypted raw content, and model-visible decrypted content.
Rotation, revocation, and rewrap are dry-run/planned only and require metadata plus audit refs.
Revoked, expired, mismatched, stale, unknown-provider, and missing-audit key refs fail closed.
Deletion policy and tombstones take precedence over rewrap, backup, export, restore, stale upload, and replay planning.
Export and backup manifests are redacted and encrypted-reference-only, with no raw ciphertext, plaintext, key material, or payload export.

Capability boundary prevents local execution, DB writes, storage writes, Builder/Oracle direct server targeting, Tier 2+ action, customer-send, deploy, trade, memory/profile commit, model-processing proof, bridge-green claim, raw restore, and server resurrection of deleted state.

Real KMS/envelope encryption can become buildable later only after KMS choice, key custody, rotation runbook, audit logging, disaster recovery, tenant isolation, red-team leakage tests, replay/watermark compatibility, operator controls, and secret storage policy exist.
Acceptance gate IDs and blocker IDs are complete and unique.

Forbidden output substrings cover secrets, key material, plaintext, raw ciphertext, raw private content, KMS/network/write/encrypt/decrypt claims, execution overclaims, bridge/proof overclaims, direct Builder/Oracle target authorization, Tier 2+ authorization overclaims, and live side-effect claims.
